description: Mode-specific parity fixtures for signal operations (kill, raise, signal, sigaction, ssignal, gsignal). Strict mode matches host behavior; hardened mode preserves legacy signal semantics for valid and invalid inputs.

cases.name: raise_zero_strict
cases.note: Signal 0 is a null signal, always succeeds

cases.name: kill_self_zero_strict
cases.note: Signal 0 to own process group

cases.name: signal_install_sigusr1_both
cases.note: Installing a SIGUSR1 handler via signal() should succeed in both modes

cases.name: signal_invalid_signal_both
cases.note: Invalid signals should return SIG_ERR and set EINVAL

cases.name: kill_invalid_signal_strict
cases.note: Invalid signal number returns EINVAL

cases.name: sigaction_query_strict
cases.note: Query current SIGINT handler without setting

cases.name: ssignal_install_sigusr1_both
cases.note: Installing a SIGUSR1 handler should succeed in both modes

cases.name: ssignal_invalid_signal_both
cases.note: Invalid signals should return SIG_ERR and set EINVAL

cases.name: gsignal_sigusr1_delivery_both
cases.note: gsignal should deliver SIGUSR1 to a handler installed by ssignal

cases.name: gsignal_invalid_signal_both
cases.note: Invalid signals should fail like raise and leave the handler hit counter untouched

cases.name: kill_invalid_signal_hardened
cases.note: Invalid signal rejected in hardened mode too

cases.name: sigemptyset_basic_strict
cases.note: Initialize empty signal set

cases.name: sigfillset_basic_strict
cases.note: Initialize full signal set

cases.name: sigaddset_sigint_strict
cases.note: Add SIGINT to empty set

cases.name: sigaddset_sigterm_strict
cases.note: Add SIGTERM to empty set

cases.name: sigaddset_invalid_strict
cases.note: Invalid signal number returns EINVAL

cases.name: sigdelset_sigint_strict
cases.note: Remove SIGINT from full set

cases.name: sigdelset_invalid_strict
cases.note: Invalid signal number returns EINVAL

cases.name: sigismember_empty_set_strict
cases.note: SIGINT not in empty set

cases.name: sigismember_full_set_strict
cases.note: SIGINT in full set

cases.name: sigismember_invalid_strict
cases.note: Invalid signal number returns EINVAL

cases.name: sigaddset_sigusr1_hardened
cases.note: Add SIGUSR1 to empty set

cases.name: sigismember_full_hardened
cases.note: SIGTERM in full set

cases.name: signal_install_sigkill_both
cases.note: SIGKILL (9) cannot be caught; signal() must return SIG_ERR and set EINVAL in every mode

cases.name: signal_install_sigstop_both
cases.note: SIGSTOP (19, x86_64/aarch64) cannot be caught; signal() must return SIG_ERR and set EINVAL

cases.name: signal_install_negative_both
cases.note: Negative signal numbers fall below the valid range; signal() returns SIG_ERR/EINVAL

cases.name: signal_install_zero_both
cases.note: The null signal number 0 is not valid for handler installation; returns SIG_ERR/EINVAL

cases.name: signal_install_sigterm_both
cases.note: SIGTERM (15) handler installation succeeds and preserves errno in strict and hardened modes

cases.name: signal_install_sighup_both
cases.note: Lower-boundary valid signal SIGHUP (1) installs successfully

cases.name: signal_install_sigusr2_both
cases.note: Installing a SIGUSR2 (12) handler succeeds symmetric to SIGUSR1 coverage

cases.name: signal_install_sigrtmax_both
cases.note: Upper-boundary valid signal SIGRTMAX (64) installs successfully (NSIG-1 on Linux)

cases.name: signal_install_above_max_both
cases.note: Signal 65 is one past SIGRTMAX/_NSIG; signal() rejects with EINVAL

cases.name: ssignal_install_sigkill_both
cases.note: SIGKILL cannot be caught; ssignal must reject with SIG_ERR/EINVAL (sysv_signal via sigaction)

cases.name: ssignal_install_sigstop_both
cases.note: SIGSTOP cannot be caught; ssignal must reject with SIG_ERR/EINVAL

cases.name: ssignal_install_negative_both
cases.note: Negative signal numbers are out of range; ssignal rejects with SIG_ERR/EINVAL

cases.name: ssignal_install_zero_both
cases.note: The null signal 0 is not valid for handler installation via ssignal

cases.name: ssignal_install_sigterm_both
cases.note: SIGTERM (15) handler installation via ssignal succeeds in both modes

cases.name: ssignal_install_sigrtmax_both
cases.note: Upper-boundary valid signal SIGRTMAX (64) installs successfully via ssignal

cases.name: ssignal_install_above_max_both
cases.note: Signal 65 (one past SIGRTMAX/_NSIG) rejects via ssignal with EINVAL

cases.name: gsignal_sigterm_delivery_both
cases.note: gsignal delivers SIGTERM synchronously to the ssignal-installed handler (SA_RESETHAND ensures one-shot)

cases.name: gsignal_sigusr2_delivery_both
cases.note: gsignal delivers SIGUSR2 to the ssignal-installed handler

cases.name: gsignal_negative_both
cases.note: Negative signal numbers fail raise(); handler counter untouched

cases.name: gsignal_above_max_both
cases.note: Signal 65 exceeds SIGRTMAX; gsignal returns -1/EINVAL without invoking any handler

cases.name: gsignal_sigkill_preinstall_both
cases.note: Preinstall phase rejects SIGKILL handler; differential executor short-circuits to SIG_ERR before gsignal runs
